Items.Title: 我停止使用 Visual Studio Code | I stopped using Visual Studio Code
Items.Description:
不是因为它不好，一点也不，老实说，我仍然将它用于我的博客🤫。

但是因为我开始使用JetBrains 产品 Webstorm并且喜欢它。

让我解释一下为什么我如此喜欢这个。 它有几个改变游戏规则的元素。

全功率但完全可定制

我认为第一次，我被所有的面板、菜单和操作压得喘不过气来。

但是，您很快就会意识到它们确实有用，但在您了解这一点之前，您可以删除面板以使其成为超级干净的编辑器。

打开基本编辑器的示例。

全部在 - 开箱即用

当我下载 WebStorm 时，我寻找了令人惊叹的插件，但很快意识到你甚至不需要它们。

它已经安装了很多开箱即用的好东西，你很少需要插件。

如果你这样做了，他们的插件生态系统也是巨大的。

开箱即用的一些示例：

  • 所有 JavaScript 自动完成，甚至可以为您解决问题
  • 智能重构
  • 测试！
  • 快速文件搜索和全局搜索
  • 实时共享内置
  • 惊人的主题
  • 包括源代码控制
  • 以及更多

如果您需要插件，请查看 Jetbrains 网站上的插件仓库。

搜索作品

我们可以使用双班搜索来搜索文件和操作。
这是一种按名称、操作、插件等查找文件的强大方法。

或者，我们会获得查找文件，可用于查找文件中的任何代码！
再次超级强大的搜索功能。

测试

如果您正在编写大量测试，WebStorm 将为您提供支持！
它提供了超级健壮的测试流程，您可以在其中快速测试单个测试用例，甚至快速查看快照。

测试单个测试或文件非常容易。
您甚至可以在 WebStorm 中快速调试您的测试。

代码检查

它附带的另一个不错的选择是代码检查，这有许多令人难以置信的好处。

它可以快速检测到您的 ESLint 规则和未使用的方法可能存在的任何问题。

它还会告诉你什么时候你在做一些没有意义的事情，比如不包括图像上的 alt 标签。

它可以帮助您编写健壮的代码的代码检查列表还在继续。

速度

它毫不犹豫地运行项目，并且重构广泛使用的导入没有问题。

自从我开始使用 WebStorm 以来，它没有出现过一个问题（现在大约三个月）。

我之前遇到过 VSC 崩溃，尤其是在打开大文件或重构广泛使用的导入文件时。

结论

我不是来告诉你必须转换的。
只是分享我切换的原因以及我在 WebStorm 中看到的好处。

明显的缺点是，与免费的 VSC 相比，它是一种付费产品。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Not because it's bad, not at all, and to be fully honest, I use it for my blog still 🤫.

But because I started using Webstorm, a JetBrains product and love it.

Let me explain why I love this one so much. There are a couple of game-changing elements to it.

Full-power but fully customizable

I think the first time, I was overwhelmed by all the panels, menus, and actions.

However, you quickly realize they do have a use, but until you learn about that, you can remove panels to make it a super clean editor.

Example of the basic editor opening up.

All in - Out of the box

One of the great features of Visual Studio Code is that it has so many amazing plugins available to make your experience better.

When I downloaded WebStorm, I looked for amazing plugins but quickly realized you don't even need them.

It has so many great things already installed out of the box that you rarely need a plugin.

And if you do, their plugin ecosystem is also huge.

Some examples of things that work out of the box:

  • All JavaScript autocomplete and can even fix issues for you
  • Smart refactoring
  • Testing!
  • Fast file search and global search
  • Live share build-in
  • Tailwind classes autocomplete
  • Amazing themes
  • Source control included
  • and much more

If you need a plugin, check out the plugin repo on the Jetbrains website.

Search works

The cool part about WebStorm is that it has perfect search functionality.

We can search for either file and actions using the double shift search.
This is a powerful way to find files by name, actions, plugins, etc.

Alternatively, we get find-in files, which can be used to find any code in your files!
Again super powerful search functionality.

Tests

If you are writing a lot of tests, WebStorm has your back!
It provides a super robust testing flow, where you can quickly test singular test cases and even quick-view snapshots.

It's super easy to test single tests or files.
You can even quickly debug your tests in WebStorm.

Code inspection

Another great option it comes with is code inspection, and this has many incredible benefits to it.

It can quickly detect any issues you might have with your ESLint rules and unused methods.

It will also tell you when you are doing things that don't make sense, like not including alt tags on images.

And the list goes on of code inspection it can do to help you write robust code.

Speed

Another great thing about WebStorm is that it's simply super quick.
It runs projects without hesitation and has no issue refactoring a widely used import.

Since I started using WebStorm, it hasn't had a single issue (about three months now).

VSC was also pretty fast to me, but WebStorm feels a bit more stable. I had VSC crash on me before, especially while opening large files or refactoring widely used imported files.

Conclusion

I'm not here to tell you that you have to switch.
Just sharing why I switched and what benefits I see in WebStorm.

The obvious con is that it's a paid product compared to VSC, which is free.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

原文:

In this article I'll tell you about a cool tool I just found on npm, called npkill, that helps you cleaning node_modules from projects you no longer user but still want to keep.

Video link: https://youtube.com/shorts/76UXyiPJQwI

Table of Contents

  • The problem
  • The solution
  • Installation
  • Options
  • Contributing
  • Conclusions

The problem

This tool is mostly for active developers who have many projects in their computer, but everyone can get benefit from it.

Everytime you run npm install or yarn on a new repository, you're basically throwing a lot of files inside the local node_modules folder.

As time goes by, you'll end by having a lot of those folders on old and dusty projects sitting there, wasting space in your disk.

For many reasons, you might want to keep those projects and not delete them, for example because of some particular implementations you want to keep as examples or template, and that's totally fine, but you don't need the node_modules folder!

The solution

I found a cool tool that helps exactly for this scenario, it's called npkill and you can find it on npm.js.

This CLI tool will navigate for your disk looking for node_modules folders and will list all of them. After the scan, you will have the power to select them one by one and hit SPACE to delete the entire folder.

Installation

As it's a regular package on npm, it's as easy as

Once you've installed in globally, you can run

and the hunt begins!

Options

The tool comes with some options you can find in the README on GitHub, but I'd like to put the spotlight in some of them that are quite useful

  • --sort or -s => Allows to sort by size or path. I think sorting by size is super handy!
  • --target or -t => Allows to set a different target folder than node_modules. You can use this option to clean up dist or build folders for example.
  • --directory or -d => Allows to specify the folder to look at. By default is the current path, but you can run it from everywhere and set a different path without repositioning your terminal.

Contributing

The repository is public and hosted in GitHub, but does not seem too active.

This doesn't mean the project is dead though, maybe they're still open for external help.

You can find the repo here: https://github.com/voidcosmos/npkill/issues

Conclusions

And that was it! I found this really handy tool by browsing the internet and cleaned up approximately 8GB on my computer, I was so happy that I wanted to share it with you!

Let me know if you already knew this tool or if you're going to use it... how much space did it save you?

Want to see it in action? Check out this YouTube #Shorts video!

Thanks for reading my post, I hope you find it interesting! Feel free to follow me to get notified when new articles are out ;)

Items.Title: 验证 PostgreSQL 结果并推断查询静态类型 | Validating PostgreSQL results & inferring query static types
Items.Description:
最初发表在我的Contra个人资料上。

大多数错误来自错位的期望。 使用数据库时，第一道防线是静态键入预期的查询结果。

 type SubscriptionPayment = { id : number , subscriber : string , amount : number }; await connection . many ( sql < Person > ` SELECT id, subscriber, amount FROM subscription_payment ` );

这样做已经极大地改善了开发人员的体验，因为现在我们可以对查询结果做出假设，甚至无需查看查询。

我们甚至可能有集成测试来进一步验证查询是否返回了预期的结果（至少在构建时）。

但是，问题在于，一旦您部署了应用程序，数据库架构可能会独立于代码库而发生变化。 这种漂移可能会导致您的应用程序以不可预测的和潜在危险的方式运行，例如，想象一下如果金额列类型从数字更改为文本。

如果没有运行时验证，这将导致一系列问题和潜在的数据库损坏。 更糟糕的是，如果没有运行时检查，这可能会在很长一段时间内被忽视。

相反，通过使用运行时检查，您可以确保始终遵守代码库和数据库之间的约定。 如果有重大更改，应用程序将失败并出现易于调试和修复的严重错误。
到目前为止，还没有一种简单的方法可以将运行时验证添加到数据库查询中。

然而，多亏了zod ，一个模式验证和静态类型干扰库，以及Slonik ，我们现在可以通过为每个查询编写一个模式并获得静态类型和运行时结果验证来实现两全其美。

如果你熟悉 JavaScript 生态系统，Relay 之于 React.js/GraphQL，Slonik 之于 Node.js/PostgreSQL。
下面是它的工作原理。

假设您有一个 PostgreSQL 表人：

 CREATE TABLE "public" . "person" ( "id" integer GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY , "name" text NOT NULL , PRIMARY KEY ( "id" ) );

并且您想要检索数据库中的所有人员，以及他们的 ID 和姓名：

 connection . any ( sql ` SELECT id, name FROM person ` );

借助您对数据库架构的了解，定义一个 zod 对象：

 const personObject = z . object ({ id : z . number (), name : z . string (), });

更新您的查询以使用 sql.type 标记并传递 personObject：

 const personQuery = sql . type ( personObject ) ` SELECT id, name FROM person ` ;

最后，使用类型化的 sql 标记模板查询数据库：

 const persons = await connection . any ( personQuery );

有了这些信息， Slonik保证 person 的每个成员都是一个具有属性 id 和 name 的对象，它们分别是非空数字和非空字符串。

处理架构验证错误

如果查询产生的行不满足 zod 对象，则抛出SchemaValidationError错误。
每当发生此错误时，日志中也会包含相同的信息。

在大多数情况下，您不应该尝试在单个查询级别处理这些错误 - 允许传播到应用程序的顶部并在您意识到问题时解决问题。

但是，在处理非结构化数据等情况下，在查询级别处理这些错误可能很有用，例如

import { SchemaValidationError } from ' slonik ' ; try {} catch ( error ) { if ( errorextendsSchemaValidationError ) { // Handle scheme validation error } }

性能损失

在网络开销的情况下，验证只占总执行时间的一小部分。只是为了说明一下，在我们的数据样本中，验证 1 行需要不到 0.1 毫秒，验证 1,000 行需要大约 3 毫秒和大约 25 毫秒验证 100,000 行。

未知键

推断类型

您可以推断查询结果的 TypeScript 类型。 有几种方法可以做到：

 // Infer using z.infer<typeof yourSchema> // https://github.com/colinhacks/zod#type-inference type Person = z . infer < typeof personObject > ; // from sql tagged template `zodObject` property type Person = z . infer < personQuery . zodObject > ;

转换结果

使用 zod 变换，您可以优化结果形状及其类型，例如

const coordinatesType = z . string (). transform (( subject ) => { const [ x , y ] = subject . split ( ' , ' ); return { x : Number ( x ), y : Number ( y )}; }); const zodObject = z . object ({ foo : coordinatesType }); const query = sql . type ( zodObject ) `SELECT '1,2' as foo` ; const result = await pool . one ( query ); expectTypeOf ( result ). toMatchTypeOf < { foo :{ x : number , y : number }} > (); t . deepEqual ( result ,{ foo :{ x : 1 , y : 2 }});

下一步是什么？

现在我们有了一个检测查询的框架，下一步是尽可能地自动化模式和类型生成。 为此，请查看 mmkal 在https://www.npmjs.com/package/@slonik/typegen中所做的工作



原文:

Originally published on my Contra profile.

Most of the bugs come from misaligned expectations. When working with databases, the first line of defense is to type the expected query results statically.

type SubscriptionPayment = { id: number, subscriber: string, amount: number
}; await connection.many( sql<Person>` SELECT id, subscriber, amount FROM subscription_payment `
);

Doing this already improves the developer experience greatly because now we can make assumptions about the query results without even looking at the query.

We might even have integration tests that further validate that the query is returning the expected results (at least at the build time).

However, the problem is that once you deploy the application, the database schema might change independently of the codebase. This drift may result in your application behaving in unpredictable and potentially dangerous ways, e.g., imagine if the amount column type changed from numeric to text.

Without the runtime validation, this would cause a cascade of problems and potential database corruption. Even worse, without runtime checks, this could go unnoticed for a long time.

In contrast, by using runtime checks, you can ensure that the contract between your codebase and the database is always respected. If there is a breaking change, the application fails with a loud error that is easy to debug and fix.
Up to now, there was not a simple way to add runtime validation to database queries.

However, thanks to zod, a schema validation and static type interference library, and Slonik, we can now have the best of both worlds by writing just a single schema per query and getting static types and runtime result validation.

If you are familiar with JavaScript ecosystem, what Relay is to React.js/GraphQL, Slonik is to Node.js/PostgreSQL.
Here is how it works.

Let's assume that you have a PostgreSQL table person:

CREATE TABLE "public"."person"( "id" integer GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY, "name" text NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

and you want to retrieve all persons in the database, along with their id and name:

connection.any(sql` SELECT id, name FROM person
`);

With your knowledge of the database schema, define a zod object:

const personObject = z.object({ id: z.number(), name: z.string(),
});

Update your query to use sql.type tag and pass personObject:

const personQuery = sql.type(personObject)` SELECT id, name FROM person
`;

Finally, query the database using typed sql tagged template:

const persons = await connection.any(personQuery);

With this information, Slonik guarantees that every member of persons is an object that has properties id and name, which are a non-null number and a non-null string respectively.

Handling schema validation errors

If query produces a row that does not satisfy zod object, then SchemaValidationError error is thrown.
SchemaValidationError includes properties that describe the query and validation errors:

  • sql – SQL of the query that produced unexpected row.
  • row – row data that did not satisfy the schema.
  • issues – array of unmet expectations.

Whenever this error occurs, the same information is also included in the logs.

In most cases, you shouldn't attempt to handle these errors at individual query level – allow to propagate to the top of the application and fix the issue when you become aware of it.

However, in cases such as dealing with unstructured data, it might be useful to handle these errors at a query level, e.g.

import { SchemaValidationError } from 'slonik'; try {} catch(error) { if (errorextendsSchemaValidationError) { // Handle scheme validation error }
}

Performance penalty

In the context of the network overhead, validation accounts for a tiny amount of the total execution time.Just to give an idea, in our sample of data, it takes sub 0.1ms to validate 1 row, ~3ms to validate 1,000 and ~25ms to validate 100,000 rows.

Unknown keys

Slonik disallows unknown keys, i.e. query that returns {foo: 'bar', baz: 'qux'} with z.object({foo: z.string()}) schema will produce SchemaValidationError error.

Inferring types

You can infer TypeScript type of the query result. There are couple of ways of doing it:

// Infer using z.infer<typeof yourSchema>
// https://github.com/colinhacks/zod#type-inference
type Person = z.infer<typeof personObject>; // from sql tagged template `zodObject` property 
type Person = z.infer<personQuery.zodObject>;

Transforming results

Using zod transform you can refine the result shape and its type, e.g.

const coordinatesType = z.string().transform((subject) => { const [x,y] = subject.split(','); return{x:Number(x),y:Number(y)};
}); const zodObject = z.object({foo:coordinatesType});
const query = sql.type(zodObject)`SELECT '1,2' as foo`;
const result = await pool.one(query);
expectTypeOf(result).toMatchTypeOf<{foo:{x:number,y:number}}>(); t.deepEqual(result,{foo:{x:1,y:2}});

What's next?

Now that we have a framework for instrumenting queries, the next step is automate schema and type generation as much as possible. For that, check out the work that mmkal has been doing in https://www.npmjs.com/package/@slonik/typegen

Items.Title: 对齐 div 中心的 4 种最简单方法 | TOP 4 Easiest Way To Align Div Center
Items.Description:
普拉卡什

发表于有四种方法可以使 div 水平和垂直居中对齐
按边距属性对齐 div

将 div 与 css 边距属性水平对齐，并且不将 div 垂直居中对齐

<html> <head>  <style>   #parent-div{    margin:20px;    width:400px;    height:150px;    border:1px solid black;  }   #child-div{    width:80px;    height:80px;    border:1px solid blue;    margin:0 auto;  }  </style> </head> <body>  <div id='parent-div'>   <div id='child-div'></div>  </div> </body> </html>

按位置属性对齐 div

通过 flex 属性对齐 div 中心

注意：当通过css flex 属性对齐div 时，您需要将flex 属性应用到其父div

 <html> <head>  <style>   #parent-div{    margin:20px;    width:400px;    height:150px;    border:1px solid black;    /* flex property apply */    display:flex;    justify-content:center; /* use for horizontally center */    align-items:center; /* use for vertically center */  }   #child-div{    width:80px;    height:80px;    border:1px solid blue;  }  </style> </head> <body>  <div id='parent-div'>   <div id='child-div'></div>  </div> </body> </html>

按网格属性对齐 div 中心

注意：当通过 css 网格属性对齐 div 时，您需要将网格属性应用到其父 div，如 css flex 属性

<html> <head>  <style>   #parent-div{    margin:20px;    width:400px;    height:150px;    border:1px solid black;    /* grid property apply */    display:grid;    justify-items:center; /* use for horizontally center */    align-items:center; /* use for vertically center */  }   #child-div{    width:80px;    height:80px;    border:1px solid blue;  }  </style> </head> <body>  <div id='parent-div'>   <div id='child-div'></div>  </div> </body> </html>

原文:

Prakash

Posted on Aug 5 • Originally published at fast2learning.com

There are four way to align div horizontally and vertically center
1 - margin

2 - position

3 - flex

4 - grid

Align div by margin property

Horizontally align div with css margin property and it not align div in vertically center

<html>
  <head>
    <style>
      #parent-div{
        margin:20px;
        width:400px;
        height:150px;
        border:1px solid black;
      }
      #child-div{
        width:80px;
        height:80px;
        border:1px solid blue;
        margin:0 auto;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id='parent-div'>
      <div id='child-div'></div>
    </div>
  </body>
</html>

Align div by position property

Div align center with horizontally and vertically by css position property

<html>
  <head>
    <style>
      #parent-div{
        margin:20px;
        width:400px;
        height:150px;
        border:1px solid black;
        position:relative;
      }
      #child-div{
        width:80px;
        height:80px;
        border:1px solid blue;
        top:50%;
        left:50%;
        transform:translate(-50% ,-50%);
        position:absolute;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id='parent-div'>
      <div id='child-div'></div>
    </div>
  </body>
</html>

Align div center by flex property

flex property use to align div horizontally and vertically center . flex is easy method above two align method

Note : when align div by css flex property , you need to apply flex property to its parent div

<html>
  <head>
    <style>
      #parent-div{
        margin:20px;
        width:400px;
        height:150px;
        border:1px solid black;
        /* flex property apply */
        display:flex;
        justify-content:center; /* use for horizontally center */
        align-items:center; /* use for vertically center */
      }
      #child-div{
        width:80px;
        height:80px;
        border:1px solid blue;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id='parent-div'>
      <div id='child-div'></div>
    </div>
  </body>
</html>

Align div center by grid property

Css grid also use to align div center from vertically and horizontally

Note : when align div by css grid property , you need to apply grid property to its parent div like css flex property

<html>
  <head>
    <style>
      #parent-div{
        margin:20px;
        width:400px;
        height:150px;
        border:1px solid black;
        /* grid property apply */
        display:grid;
        justify-items:center; /* use for horizontally center */
        align-items:center; /* use for vertically center */
      }
      #child-div{
        width:80px;
        height:80px;
        border:1px solid blue;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id='parent-div'>
      <div id='child-div'></div>
    </div>
  </body>
</html>

Items.Title: 缓存 Web 和数据库第 3 部分 | Caching Web and Databases Part-3
Items.Description:
即使每秒有数百万个请求，这也将性能从毫秒到微秒提高了 10 倍，DAX 将完成向 DynamoDB 表添加内存加速所需的所有繁重工作，而无需开发人员管理任何一种缓存验证数据填充或集群管理。 使用它有很多好处，因为它是一个完全托管的组件。 这意味着它将具有高度可扩展性、灵活性和安全性。 它提供了极致的性能，因为这些东西将内置到组件本身中。 因为它是完全托管的，并且因为它与 DynamoDB 集成，所以非常易于使用。 因此，一旦完成所有设置和配置，数据将开始写入 DynamoDB，缓存也将生效，读取将从缓存中进行，以便获得更快的响应时间。

亚马逊 ElastiCache

因此，ElastiCache 是一种在 AWS 中可用的内存数据存储，它允许您在云中的内存数据存储中无缝设置运行和扩展流行的开源兼容存储，并且由于它是一种 Web 服务，因此易于部署、操作和它还允许您在两个内存数据库引擎之间进行选择，即 Memcached（一种广泛采用的内存对象缓存系统）和 Redis（一种流行的开源内存键值存储）。 支持排序集和列表等数据结构。 它用于从内存数据存储中的高吞吐量和低延迟中检索数据，它显着改善了许多读取繁重的应用程序的延迟和吞吐量。 社交媒体、游戏或媒体共享等工作负载通过将关键数据存储在内存中以实现低延迟访问，从而提高应用程序性能 Amazon ElastiCache 也是缓存会话存储游戏、地理空间服务、实时使用案例等实时用例的流行选择。时间分析和排队。 它提供了完全托管的渲染，在 Stack Overflow 2020 开发人员调查中为开发人员最喜爱的数据库和最苛刻的应用程序提供了 Memcached。 这需要亚毫秒级的响应时间。 此外，您还可以根据需要在 Radius 和 Memcached 之间进行选择，它们具有某些特殊功能，例如用于 Radius 的 ElastiCache 具有高可用性，而用于 Memcached 的 ElastiCache 是多线程的。 所以两者都有一定的相似之处，都有一定的区别。 这完全取决于您的用例，您必须在 ElastiCache 中使用什么引擎。 您可以为半径选择 ElastiCache，也可以选择带有 Memcached 的 ElastiCache。


让我们了解 ElastiCache 对所有互联网规模应用程序的高级工作，例如游戏中的实时应用程序、媒体流、约会、需要快速访问的社交媒体，以及所有实时交易、聊天等用例应用程序 BI 和分析会话、存储游戏排行榜和缓存 ElastiCache 创造奇迹，它位于您的 Web 服务器和数据库之间，通过从缓存本身提供请求来更快地处理请求。 它也可以用作消息代理，并将临时数据或临时数据存储在内存中以便快速响应。现在让我们看一下 ElastiCache 的一些用例。 首先，我们可以将 ElastiCache 与我们现有的数据库一起使用来缓存经常访问的记录，因为它的低延迟和高吞吐量，它很容易加载到您的关系数据库或 NoSQL 数据库（Redis 或 Memcached 引擎）。 它还可以与 Amazon S3 一起使用，此外，ElastiCache for Redis 可以提供查询结果缓存、持久会话缓存和整页缓存。 除此之外，它还可用于管理 Redis 和 Memcached 的会话信息。 包括游戏、电子商务网站和社交媒体平台在内的在线应用程序通常需要会话管理。 除此之外，在机器学习方面，我们还可以使用 ElastiCache 减少使用案例，例如游戏中的欺诈检测和金融服务中 AdTech 中的实时竞价以及约会和拼车中的匹配，以处理实时数据并在几十秒内做出决策在流解决方案中除此之外的毫秒数，我们可以使用 ElastiCache 来减少流解决方案，例如 Apache Kafka 和 Amazon Kinesis 作为存储来摄取处理和分析实时数据，并且 ElastiCache 可用于实时分析用例，例如作为针对个性化物联网和时间序列数据分析的社交媒体广告。

原文:

DAX Amazon DynamoDB accelerator or in the short form you can also call it as DAX is a fully managed highly available in-memory cache for Amazon DynamoDB. That delivers up to a 10 times improvement in the performance from milliseconds to microseconds even at millions of requests per second and DAX will do all the heavy lifting that is required to add in-memory acceleration to the DynamoDB tables without requiring the developers to manage any kind of caching validation data population or cluster management. There are a lot of benefits of using that since it is a fully managed component. Which means it will be highly scalable, flexible and secure. It is delivering extreme performance all because these things will be built into the component itself. Since it is fully managed and it's really easy to use because it is integrated with DynamoDB. DAX is designed to run within an Amazon VPC environment and you can just launch a DAX cluster in your virtual network and control the access to the cluster by using the security groups of the VPC.

So, once it is all set up and configured the data will start getting written to the DynamoDB and the cache will also be into action and the reads will be happening from the cache so as to get the faster response times.

Amazon ElastiCache

So, ElastiCache is a in memory data store that is available in AWS it allows you to seamlessly set up run and scale popular open source compatible in memory data stores in the cloud and since it is a web service it is easy to deploy, operate and scale and also it allows you to choose between two in-memory database engines that is Memcached which is a widely adopted memory object caching system and the Redis which is a popular open source in-memory key value store. That supports data structure such as sorted sets and the list. It is used for retrieving the data from high throughput and low latency in memory data stores and it significantly improved the latency and throughput for many read heavy applications. Workloads for example social media, gaming or media sharing improving the application performance by storing critical pieces of data in memory for low latency access also Amazon ElastiCache is a popular choice for real-time use cases like caching session stores gaming, geospatial services, real-time analytics and queuing. It offers fully managed renders which is watered the most loved database by developers in the stack overflow 2020 developer survey and the Memcached for your most demanding applications. That require sub millisecond response times. Also you can choose between Radius and Memcached based on your needs there are certain special features with both like ElastiCache for Radius is highly available and ElastiCache for Memcached is multi-threaded. So both have certain similarities both have certain differences. It totally depends on your use case that what engine you have to use with your ElastiCache. You can go for ElastiCache for radius or you can go for ElastiCache with Memcached.


Let's understand the high level working of the ElastiCache for all the internet scale applications like real-time applications in the gaming, media streaming, dating, social media that needs fast access and also for the use cases like all the real-time transactions, chat applications BI and analytics session, store gaming leaderboards and caching ElastiCache works wonders it sits between your web servers and the database to serve the request faster by serving them from the cache itself. It can also be used as a message broker and it stores ephemeral or the temporary data in memory for the quick responses now let's have a look at a few of the use cases of ElastiCache. First of all we can use ElastiCache with our existing database to cache the frequently accessed records it will easily load of your relational or the NoSQL database either Redis or Memcached engine because of its low latency and the high throughput. It can also be used with Amazon S3 and additionally ElastiCache for Redis can deliver query results caching persistent session caching and full page caching. Apart from this it can be used to manage a session information for both Redis and Memcached. The session management is commonly required for the online applications including games, e-commerce website and the social media platforms. Apart from this in terms of machine learning we can use ElastiCache for reduce in the use cases such as fraud detection in gaming and financial services real time bidding in AdTech and match making in dating and ride sharing to process live data and make the decisions within tens of milliseconds apart from this in streaming solutions we can use ElastiCache for reduce with the streaming solutions such as Apache Kafka and Amazon Kinesis as a store to ingest process and analyze real-time data and ElastiCache can be used for real-time analytic use cases such as social media Ad targeting personalization IOT and the time series data analytics.

Items.Title: Android中viewModel中的资源（R.string） | Resources(R.string) in viewModel in Android
Items.Description:
桑迪普卫星

发表于我想分享一种技术来访问视图模型中运行良好的字符串。 所以有多种方法可以在视图模型中访问字符串，但它们各有优缺点。

我们有这样的选择

  1. 在代码中硬编码一个字符串，但这在可重用性和字符串本地化的情况下无济于事
  2. 使用活动的上下文，但在 mvvm 中，在视图模型中直接使用上下文是不好的做法
  3. 返回整数的字符串资源 id 并在活动中获取实际的字符串，但这会增加工作量

让我们朝着解决方案前进
在 MVVM 中，hilt 将有额外的好处来克服上述问题。

我们将添加 StringResourcesProvider.kt

 @Singleton class StringResourcesProvider @Inject constructor( @ApplicationContext private val context: Context ) { fun getString(@StringRes stringResId: Int): String { return context.getString(stringResId) } }

定义视图模型以获取字符串

@HiltViewModel class AuthViewModel @Inject constructor( private val stringResourcesProvider: StringResourcesProvider ) : ViewModel() { ... fun validate() { val username: String = stringResourcesProvider.getString(R.string.username) } ... }

通过这种方式，我们可以在视图模型或任何其他地方访问字符串。

感谢您阅读我的帖子。

Posted on Aug 5

I would like to share a technique to access the strings in view model that works well. So there are multiple ways to access the string in view model but they have there own advantages and disadvantages.

We have options like

  1. Hardcode a string in code but this will not help in case of reusability and string localisation
  2. Use the context of an activity but in mvvm its bad practice to use context direct in view model
  3. Return the string resource id which is integer and in activity get the actual string but this will increase the efforts

Let's move toward the solution
In MVVM and hilt will have addition benefits to overcome above issue.

We will add the StringResourcesProvider.kt

@Singleton
class StringResourcesProvider @Inject constructor(
    @ApplicationContext private val context: Context
) {
    fun getString(@StringRes stringResId: Int): String {
        return context.getString(stringResId)
    }
}

Define the view model to get the string

@HiltViewModel
class AuthViewModel @Inject constructor(
    private val stringResourcesProvider: StringResourcesProvider
) : ViewModel() {
    ...
    fun validate() {
        val username: String = stringResourcesProvider.getString(R.string.username)
    }
    ...
}

In this way we can access the string in view model or any other places.

Thanks for reading my post.
Happy codding

Items.Title: Python 中的可迭代解包 #PyTip02 | Iterable Unpacking in Python #PyTip02
Items.Description:
其中最常见的是list和tuple 。 这些序列有时用于存储记录，稍后需要对其进行解包。

从序列中解包值时，您可能会遇到诸如ValueError: too many values to unpack之类的错误。 在这篇简短的文章中，我将分享一个简短的 PyTip 以避免此错误。

问题

在 Python REPL 中创建一个文件或运行以下代码。

 primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11] first, second, third = primes

上面的代码会产生以下错误。

 Traceback (most recent call last): File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module> ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 3)

上述错误表明，要解压您的可迭代对象，您的可迭代对象大小必须为 3。

解决方案

为了解决这个问题，提出了PEP 3132 。 现在，在 Python REPL 中编写以下代码以查看扩展可迭代解包的实际效果。

 >>> primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11] >>> first, second, third, *_ = primes >>> print(first, second, third, _) 2 3 5 [7, 11]

在上面的输出中，您可以看到*_语法从可迭代对象中捕获了所有多余的值。 此外，“*_”可以在语句中的任何位置使用。 考虑下面的例子来理解这个想法。

 >>> primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11] >>> first, *_ , last = primes >>> print(first, last, _) 2 11 [3, 5, 7]

我发现这个功能非常有用，希望你也觉得它有用。 感谢您阅读今天的 PyTip。

原文:

Python provides an awesome variety of sequential data structures. The most common of them are list and tuple. These sequences are sometimes used to store records, which later needs to be unpacked.

When unpacking the values from a sequence you may encounter error like-ValueError: too many values to unpack. In this short post I will share a short PyTip to avoid this error.

Problem

Create a file or run the below code in the Python REPL.

primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11]
first, second, third = primes

The above code will produce the following error.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 3)

The above error states that to unpack your iterable, your iterable size must be three.

Solution

To solve this problem PEP 3132 was proposed. Now, write the following code in Python REPL to see Extended Iterable Unpacking in action.

>>> primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11]
>>> first, second, third, *_ = primes
>>> print(first, second, third, _)
2 3 5 [7, 11]

In the above output you can see that *_ syntax caught all the excess values from the iterable. The _ variable could be anything of your choice, it doesn't have to be an underscore. Additionally, "*_" may be used anywhere in the statement. Consider the example below to get the idea.

>>> primes = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11]
>>> first, *_ , last = primes
>>> print(first, last, _)
2 11 [3, 5, 7]

I find this feature extreamly useful and hope you find it useful too. Thanks for the reading today's PyTip.

Items.Title: 投资组合项目 4：使用 Javascript 的 Fish Store | Portfolio Project 4: The Fish Store Using Javascript
Items.Description:
我终于到达了 Javascript 模块的末尾！ 发生了一些重大的生活变化，导致该模块的使用寿命比我预期的要长得多。

这些变化包括连续几个月每周工作 60 到 80 小时，周末待命，然后通宵待命。 在过去的几年里，这项工作已经非常苛刻，今年大幅增加。 这让我几乎没有时间和精力在我的课堂上工作，也让我的家人有他们应得的时间。 在这家公司工作了 6 年多后，我决定在 2022 年 7 月下旬辞职，因为我不再对自己的工作感到快乐，这阻碍了我的生活和职业目标。 因此，向前迈进，我将全身心地投入到完成我的课程并追求我想要的软件工程职业。

对于我的 Javascript Portfolio 项目，我创建了一个名为 The Fish Store 的 Web 应用程序。 当我在高中的时候，我有多个咸水鱼缸（都是 90 加仑）并且很享受这个爱好。 我记得当时鱼类信息和商店的网站在设计和功能上都非常简陋。 回想这段时间，我认为创建一个概念验证将为这个社区/爱好带来更好的用户体验将是一个很棒的“改进项目”。

对于这个项目，我们必须创建一个在服务器（后端）上运行的 ruby​​ on rails API，并且 HTML/Javascript 前端应用程序将从中提取。 由于这种设计，我希望 Web 应用程序尽可能动态，因为随着后台 API 数据库的增长，Web 应用程序需要足够动态以处理不断增长的数据库。

我想出的是每条鱼的磁贴/卡片设计以及过滤功能，以便能够轻松地对特定类别的鱼进行分类。 我用于此爱好的许多旧网站都将鱼按类别划分，因此保持相似的结构，我认为这是有道理的，我创建了一个过滤器功能，仅显示属于鱼的图块那个类别。

我对现有网站的另一个改进是我使用视频来展示鱼，而不仅仅是图片。 图片有时可能会产生误导，并且有一段视频可以展示鱼的移动和外观，可以创造更好的用户体验。 我承认，如果您在一个页面上有太多的瓷砖/卡片和太多的视频，可能会减慢网站的速度并需要它使用大量资源。 我想象的是，随着这个网站的发展，如果它在使用中，开发人员会限制单个页面上的磁贴数量，并允许用户一次只加载特定的数量。 然后会有一个“下一页”元素，它不会在物理上将您引导到新页面，而是会异步加载下一组图块。

我将 HTML 文件保持得非常小，只有标题和“订购请求”是 HTML 文件生成的唯一 HTML 元素。 其他一切都将由 Javascript 代码处理。

在创建 Javascript 代码以动态处理图块时，我确实遇到了一些挑战。 我对这些挑战一点也不沮丧，因为这个项目肯定有助于加强我对 Javascript 的理解，因为它需要大量的调试和重新编码才能让一切正常工作。

在开发应用程序的过程中，我确实不得不退后一步。 最初，当页面最初加载时，我使用一个 Fetch 调用来获取 JSON 结构，然后我根据用户导航页面来隐藏/显示内容。 我意识到这会导致一些麻烦的资源使用，也使编码更加混乱。 一个例子是所有视频都在加载和运行，但只是隐藏在后台。 我决定最好在用户浏览应用程序时创建和删除元素，这有助于我保持代码更简洁。

特别是一项挑战给了我很多问题。 这个挑战是为每条鱼设置评论页面：

  1. 在磁贴中加载新的评论表单
  2. 在磁贴中加载现有评论
  3. 允许从 5 星中选择您的评分
  4. 异步发布并显示新提交的评论

弄清楚 5 星评审过程是令人愉快的，因为这是新的，而不是课堂上涵盖的内容。 需要一些研究和思考才能使其正常工作。 我不得不查找如何添加星形以及如何使用 CSS 来填充星形。 然后我必须创建一个循环来生成不是一颗，而是五颗星，并为每颗星创建一个监听器。 它还从功能的角度考虑，因为如果我单击第四颗星，那么我希望从一到三的星也自动填写并将星数保存到 API 数据库。 此外，如果用户不小心点击了五颗星，但本来打算放四颗星，并且在提交评论之前发现了这个错误，他们将需要一种允许用户调整星星的方法。 当我让它正确显示和运行时，我感到非常自豪。 为了让星星工作，我必须执行以下操作：

位于 HTML 文件中，我需要添加一个到 Font Awesome 的链接。

 <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css">

我使用 Javascript 代码来创建星星。

 for (let k = 1; k < 6; k++){ let starDiv = createDiv('fa fa-star', k) element = starsDiv.appendChild(starDiv) if (k <= review.stars){ starDiv.className = 'fa fa-star checked' } }

在 CSS 文件中设置以下内容。

 .checked { color: orange; }

我在开发评论页面时遇到的最具挑战性的事情是提交新评论并让它立即使用添加的评论更新页面，这样用户就可以看到他们的贡献而无需重新加载网页。 初始设置本身并不困难，但由于每个图块都与其他图块异步运行，因此我在以下情况下遇到了错误：

  1. 打开一条鱼的评论页面，我将其命名为 Fish #1。
  2. 打开第二条鱼的评论页面，我将其命名为 Fish #2。
  3. 提交对 Fish #1 的评论。 这造成了一些数据完整性问题，因为变量/参数会从打开 Fish #2 评论页面（最后打开的页面）转移到 Fish #1 评论提交中。 当用户能够独立操作所有图块时，需要认真思考和逐步排除故障来调试代码并使代码重新加载正确的目标 Fish 变量/参数。 这就是 console.log() 函数有很大帮助的地方，因为它可以被插入以查看变量/参数信息并验证代码的流程。

我将页面的功能集中在使用 Javascript 的用户体验上，并且不包括管理页面或在线订购/购物车功能。 我们过去在课堂的其他阶段已经这样做了，我相信我将在我的期末项目中拥有完整的功能。

我对课程的最后阶段和我的最终项目感到非常兴奋！ 也就是说，到目前为止，Javascript 部分一直是我的最爱，因为您在显示正确数据、Javascript 的设计方面以及使用它创建非常动态的 Web 应用程序的能力方面遇到了挑战。



原文:

I have finally reached the end of the Javascript module! Had some significant life changes that caused this module to last much longer than I expected.

Those changes involved working 60 to 80 hour weeks for several months, being on call on the weekends, and then being on call overnight. The job had already been very demanding over the last couple of years significantly ramped up this year. This left very little time and energy to work on my class and also give my family the time they deserve. After working at that company for over 6 years, I decided to resign at the end of July 2022, as I was no longer finding joy in what I was doing and it was impeding my personal goals for my life and my career. So moving forward I am throwing myself into finishing my class and pursuing the career that I want, in Software Engineering.

For my Javascript Portfolio project, I created a web application called The Fish Store. When I was in high school I had multiple saltwater fish tanks (both 90 gallons) and enjoyed the hobby. I remember back then that the websites for fish information and stores were pretty rudimentary in design and functionality. Thinking back to this time, I thought it would be a great "improvement project" to create a proof of concept that would bring a better user experience to this community/hobby.

For this project we had to create a ruby on rails API that would run on a server (back-end) and the HTML/Javascript front-end application would pull from it. Due to this design, I wanted the web application to be as dynamic as possible because as the background API database grows, the web application would need to be dynamic enough to handle the growing database.

What I came up with is a tile/card design for each fish along with a filter feature to be able to easily sort for fish in a certain category. Many of the older websites that I used to use for this hobby had the fish divided by category, so keeping with the similar structure, which I think makes sense, I created a filter feature that would only display the tiles of the fish that belong to that category.

Another improvement I made over existing websites is that I use video to show the fish, rather than just a picture. Pictures can sometimes be misleading and having a video showing how the fish moves and looks, creates a better user experience. I do admit that if you have too many tiles/cards on a single page with too many videos could slow down the website and require it to use a lot of resources. What I imagine is that as this website would evolve, if it was in use, the developers would limit the number of tiles on a single page and allow a user to load only a specific number at time. Then there would be a "next page" element which would not physically direct you to a new page, but would load the next set of tiles asynchronously.

I kept the HTML file very minimal, with only the title and the "Call for ordering" being the only HTML elements generated by the HTML file. Everything else would be handled by the Javascript code.

I definitely did run into some challenges when creating the Javascript code to dynamically handle the tiles. I'm not upset at all about the challenges, because this project definitely helped reinforce my understanding of Javascript as it took a significant amount of debugging and re-coding to get everything to work correctly.

During the process of developing the application, I did have to take a step back. Originally I was using one Fetch call to get the JSON structure when the page was initially loaded and then I was hiding/showing things based on the user navigating the page. I realized that this lead to some cumbersome resource use and also made the coding more messy. One example being that all videos were being loaded and running, but would just be hidden in the background. I decided it was best to create and remove elements as the user navigated the application, which helped me keep my code cleaner.

One challenge in particular gave me a lot of issues. This challenge was the setup of the Review page for each fish:

  1. Loads a new review form in the tile
  2. Loads existing reviews in the tile
  3. Allows selecting of your rating out of 5 stars
  4. Asynchronously posts and displays a newly submitted review

Figuring out the 5 star review process was enjoyable as this was new and not something that had been covered in the class. It took some research and thought to get this working correctly. I had to look up how to add a star and how to use CSS to fill the star. Then I had to create a loop to generate not one, but five stars, and a listener for each star. It also took thought from a functionality standpoint because if I click on the fourth star, then I would want stars one through three to also automatically fill in and also save the star count to the API database. Also if a user accidentally clicked five stars, but meant to put four stars, and caught this mistake before submitting the review, they would need a way to allow the user to adjust the stars. I was quite proud when I got this to display and function correctly. To get the stars working I had to do the following:

Located in HTML file, I need to add a link to Font Awesome.

<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css">

I used the Javascript code to create the stars.

for (let k = 1; k < 6; k++){
          let starDiv = createDiv('fa fa-star', k)
          element = starsDiv.appendChild(starDiv)
          if (k <= review.stars){
            starDiv.className = 'fa fa-star checked'
          }
      }

Setup the following in the CSS file.

.checked {
  color: orange;
}

The most challenging thing I ran into with the development of the Review page was doing the submission of the new review and having it immediately update the page with the added review so the user can see their contribution without having to reload the webpage. The initial setup itself was not difficult, but since each tile runs asynchronously from the others, I ran into bugs when I would:

  1. Open the Review page on a fish, which I will call Fish #1.
  2. Open the Review page on a second fish, which I will call Fish #2.
  3. Submit a review on Fish #1. This created some data integrity issues because the variables/arguments would carry over from opening the Fish #2 review page (last page opened) into the Fish #1 review submission. It took some serious thought and step-by-step troubleshooting to debug the code and make the code re-load the correct target Fish variables/arguments when the user has the ability to manipulate all tiles independent of each other. That is where console.log() function helps considerably as it can be inserted to see variable/argument information and to verify process flow of the code.

I kept the functionality of the page focused on the user experience with Javascript and did not include an admin page or online ordering/cart functionality. We have done that in the past in other phases of the class and I'm sure I will be having full blown functionality in my final project.

I am very excited for the final Phase of the course and my final project! That said, the Javascript portion has been my favorite so far because of the challenges you run into with displaying correct data, the design aspect of Javascript, and the ability to create a very dynamic web application with it.

To achieve backward compatibility, sometimes we have to write separate code for different 𝗡𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗷𝘀 versions.

This happens as a result of APIs getting deprecated and others emerging as their official replacements.

Fortunately, there are ways to handle this issue. Today I'll be talking about one.

涉及哪些步骤？

第 1 步：在 Google Developers Cloud 上创建一个新项目

  1. 转到Google Developers Cloud并选择“我的第一个项目”

  2. 这将打开一个弹出窗口。 在此弹出窗口的右上角选择“新建项目”。

  3. 选择一个唯一的项目名称，例如 new-tutorial。 您可以保留“组织”字段，然后单击“创建”

第 2 步：启用 Google 表格和 Google Drive API

  1. 在仪表板上，单击左侧窗格中的“库”。 这会将您重定向到 API 库。

  2. 搜索谷歌表格。 这会将您重定向到 Google 表格 API 窗口。

  3. 继续单击“启用 API”。

按照上述步骤也启用 Google Drive API。

请注意，必须为您创建的每个新项目启用 2 个 API。

第 3 步：创建凭据

注意：始终检查您是否仍在处理正确的项目

  1. 在左侧窗格中，选择“凭据”

  2. 在打开的窗口中选择“+ Create Credentials”

  3. 选择“服务帐户” 。 服务帐户是一种特殊类型的 Google 帐户，旨在代表需要进行身份验证并被授权访问 Google API [原文如此] 中的数据的非人类用户。

  4. 在服务帐户详细信息 - 对于服务帐户名称- 您可以选择与您的项目相似的名称。 例如新教程。 服务帐户 ID将自动更新。 您可以选择对其进行编辑。 对于Service Account Description ，您也可以选择将其留空。

  5. 选择“创建并继续”

  6. 在“授予此服务帐户对项目的访问权限”上，选择一个角色作为所有者，以便您拥有完整的权限。

  7. 点击“继续”

  8. 在“Grant users access to this service account ”中，您可以添加其他用户或组。 如果您没有其他用户，则可以将其留空。 下面是一些用户或组的示例。

     a. Google Account email: [user@gmail.com](mailto:user@gmail.com), b. Google Group: admins@googlegroups.com, c. Service account: server@example.gserviceaccount.com d. Google Workspace domain: example.com

  9. 点击“完成”继续

  10. 在新打开的窗口中，页面底部有一个服务帐户部分，选择左侧的“管理服务帐户” 。

  11. 在桌子上，单击末尾的 3 个点，在“操作部分”上。

  12. 选择“管理密钥”。

  13. 单击“添加密钥”并选择“创建新密钥”。

  14. 选择 JSON 作为密钥类型。

  15. 将自动下载 JSON。 它应该如下所示：-

注意：确保 JSON 文件保存在与您的项目相同的文件夹中。

第 4 步：创建 Google 表格

  1. 转到 Google 表格并创建一个新表格。

  2. 单击“共享”按钮并添加 JSON 文件中的客户端电子邮件，例如

从 URL 获取电子表格密钥。 如下图黑色突出显示。

此电子表格密钥将在笔记本中使用，如下所示。

第 5 步：Jupyter 笔记本

回到笔记本。

导入必要的库

# import libraries import gspread from oauth2client.service_account import ServiceAccountCredentials from df2gspread import df2gspread as d2g

访问 Google 表格和 Google Drive API

将数据框上传到 Google 表格的代码。

Claire Maina

Posted on Aug 5

The main benefit of connecting your Jupyter notebook with Tableau is that this allows for real-time analysis. To mean that, any changes made to the notebook will be reflected on Google Sheets and Tableau.

What are the steps involved?

1: Create a New Project on Google Developers Cloud

2: Enable Google Sheets and Google Drive APIs

3: Create credentials

4: Google Sheets

5: Code to add to your Jupyter Notebook

6: Tableau Public setup

Step 1: Create a New Project on Google Developers Cloud

  1. Go to Google Developers Cloud and select “My First Project”

  2. This will open a pop-up window. Select “New Project” on the top right side of this pop-up window.

  3. Choose a unique Project Name e.g. new-tutorial. You may leave the Organization field as it is and click on Create

Step 2: Enable Google Sheets and Google Drive APIs

  1. On the Dashboard, click “Library”, on the Left Pane. This will redirect you to API Library.

  2. Search for Google Sheets. This will redirect you to the Google sheets API window.

  3. Proceed to click on “Enable API”.

Follow the above steps to also Enable the Google Drive API.

Note that the 2 APIs have to be enabled for each new project you create.

Step 3: Create credentials

Note: Always check that you are still working on the correct project

  1. On the left pane, select “Credentials”

  2. On the opened window select “+ Create Credentials”

  3. Select “Service Account”. A service account is a special type of Google account intended to represent a non-human user that needs to authenticate and be authorized to access data in Google APIs [sic].

  4. On Service Account Details - for the Service Account Name - you can choose a name similar to your project. E.g. new-tutorial. The Service Account ID will automatically update. You may choose to edit it. For the Service Account Description, you may choose to leave this blank as well.

  5. Select “Create and Continue”

  6. On “Grant this service account access to project”, select a role as Owner so that you may have full rights.

  7. Click “Continue”

  8. On “Grant users access to this service account”, you may add other users or groups. If you do not have other users, you may leave it blank. Below are some examples of users or groups.

    a. Google Account email: [user@gmail.com](mailto:user@gmail.com),

b. Google Group: admins@googlegroups.com,

c. Service account: server@example.gserviceaccount.com

d. Google Workspace domain: example.com

  9. Click “Done” to proceed

  10. On the newly opened window, there is a Service Account section at the bottom of the page, select “Manage Service Accounts” on the left.

  11. On the table, click on the 3 dots at the end, on the ”Actions sections”.

  12. Select “Manage Keys”.

  13. Click “Add Key” and select “Create New Key”.

  14. Select JSON as the Key Type.

  15. A JSON will be automatically downloaded. It should be like the below:-

Note: Ensure the JSON file is saved in the same folder as your project.

Step 4: Create a Google Sheet

  1. Go to Google Sheets and create a new one.

  2. Click the Share button and add the client-email that was on the JSON file e.g.

"new-tutorial@ new-tutorial-...iam.gserviceaccount.com"

Get the spreadsheet key from the URL. As highlighted below in black.

"://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1l9yMNyoXt.....cZCYACyc/edit#gid=0 "

This Spreadsheet Key will be used in the notebook as shown below.

Step 5: Jupyter Notebook

Back to the notebook.

Import the necessary libraries

# import libraries

import  gspread

from  oauth2client.service_account  import  ServiceAccountCredentials

from  df2gspread  import  df2gspread  as  d2g

Access Google Sheets and Google Drive APIs

# Access the Google Sheets and Google Drive APIs

scope = ['https://spreadsheets.google.com/feeds','https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive']

credentials = ServiceAccountCredentials.from_json_keyfile_name('name_of_your_json_file.json',scopes=scope)

gc = gspread.authorize(credentials)

Code to upload the dataframe to Google Sheets.

spreadsheet_key is, what we copied earlier when we created the Google Sheet.

worksheet_name is, by default named Sheet_1. You can change this on the Google sheet as per your preference.

# Share the dataframe with Google Sheets

spreadsheet_key = '1XccW9EiPSYHRmmESyLTTUnmRdfMDiGlzqnG0_jOmA5Q'

worksheet_name = "Main"

result = d2g.upload(your_dataframe_name, spreadsheet_key, worksheet_name, credentials = credentials, row_names=True)

print(result)

Step 6: Tableau

To download Tableau click here

  1. On the left pane, select “Google Drive”

  1. This will require authentication and it will redirect you to your browser.

  2. Enter your correct details and select Allow.

  3. A pop-up window will appear, where you will select the correct project Google Sheet, as you had saved it.

  4. This will open your data in Tableau as shown below: -

Items.Title: 第一篇博文……永远。 | First blog post...ever.
Items.Description:
你好！ 热烈欢迎来到我作为有抱负的软件开发人员的第一篇博客文章 - 我目前正在熨斗学校学习编码。 我的目标是使用这个博客来跟踪我在编码世界中的成长和进步。

全面披露：我对编码的了解可能与新司机了解她的汽车引擎盖下的东西一样多。 我只知道如何从 A 点到达 B 点——让代码做我想做的事——但对如何让汽车更有效地使用汽油或它甚至使用什么部件一无所知。

为了了解我所处的位置，我最近意识到的一件事——这对我来说是一个很大的垫脚石——是能够将变量分配给已经定义的对象（作为指针）以进行修改到不同范围内的所述对象。

为了显示：

 let variable = {} //globally defining an empty object function parentFunction(functionName) { const dataObj = { key1: "I want to change this value.", key2: "I want to keep this value.", } variable = dataObj //re-assigning (or pointing) our empty object to the defined object functionName(variable) console.log(dataObj) //display our defined object after callback function has been called }; function callbackFunction(object) { object.key1 = "This value has been changed." } parentFunction(callbackFunction)

然后，在我们的控制台日志中，我们将看到我们定义的对象已更新为：

 { key1: 'This value has been changed.', key2: 'I want to keep this value.' }

请注意，传递给回调函数的参数是variable ，而不是实际的dataObj 。 然而，更改仍然存在于dataObj上。 这对我来说是一个突破，因为我在一个随机的晚上试图入睡时想到了这个想法。 希望它可以对遇到此问题的任何新开发人员有用！

另外，快速记下我从这个项目中学到的另一个技巧：
要替换字符串中的space字符，简单的使用：

 string.replace(/\s/g,"<new character you want to replace with>")

谢谢阅读！

原文:

Hello! Warm welcome to my very first blog post as an aspiring software developer - where I'm currently learning to code at the Flatiron School. I aim to use this blog to track my growth and progress in the coding world.

Full disclosure: I probably know about as much of coding as a brand-new driver knows what's under the hood of her car. I simply know how to get from point A to point B -- make the code do what I want it to -- but nothing on how to make the car use gas more efficiently or what parts it's even using at all.

To get an idea of where I'm at, one thing I realized most recently - that was a big stepping stone for me - is the ability to assign a variable to an already-defined object (as a pointer) in order to make modifications to said object in a different scope.

To illustrate:

let variable = {} //globally defining an empty object

function parentFunction(functionName) {
    const dataObj = {
        key1: "I want to change this value.",
        key2: "I want to keep this value.",
    }

    variable = dataObj //re-assigning (or pointing) our empty
object to the defined object

    functionName(variable)
    console.log(dataObj) //display our defined object after
callback function has been called
};

function callbackFunction(object) {
    object.key1 = "This value has been changed."
}

parentFunction(callbackFunction)

Then, in our console log, we'll see our defined-object has been updated to:

{
  key1: 'This value has been changed.',
  key2: 'I want to keep this value.'
}

Note the argument that was passed into the callback function was the variable, not the actual dataObj. Yet the changes persisted on the dataObj. This was a breakthrough for me as the thought came to my head as I was trying to fall asleep one random night. Hope it can be of use to any new developers that come across this!

Also, quick note on another trick I learned from this project:
To replace the space character in a string, simple use:

string.replace(/\s/g,"<new character you want to replace with>")

Thanks for reading!

Items.Title: 通过解组 Go 结构将微服务 JSON 响应中继到客户端 | Relaying a Microservice JSON Response to the Client by Unmarshalling Go Structs
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我们将通过添加诸如名称、日期或唯一标识符之类的元数据并将其中继到用户客户端来转换来自微服务的 JSON 响应。 这是我在 iOS 上为研究人员构建 AI SaaS 应用程序的一部分，作为示例，我们将假装将数据发送到动物园动物的 AI 对象标识符。

过去一周，我一直致力于将图像分析微服务连接到用户前端。 在这篇文章中，我将讨论如何转换 JSON 响应并添加元数据，如名称、日期或唯一标识符。 您还可以转换数据，因此我们可以将 X-Min 中继为坐标和width而不是x_max ，而不是转发x_min和x_max坐标。 为了在客户端区分对象是由算法还是人类检测到，我们添加了一个新的字段method 。 这也可能是一个更复杂的对象来存储 AI 算法的版本。 中继的响应应如下所示

{ "name" : "Zoo Image Analysis" , "xid" : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" "predictions" : [ { "confidence" : 0.69146144 , "label" : "Lion" , "x" : 682 , "y" : 109 , "width" : 77 , // TODO calculate actual width "height" : 25 , "method" : "AI" }, { "confidence" : 0.7 , "label" : "Penguin" , "x" : 782 , "y" : 209 , "width" : - 13 , "height" : - 65 , "method" : "AI" }] }

为此，我们将创建一个ResponseAdapter结构来模仿 AI 微服务的响应结构以及PredictionAdapter 。 然后我们创建一个Response和一个Prediction结构，其中包含我们想要传递给客户端的字段和方法。

  1. 使用空接口打印未知 JSON 数据
  2. 将 JSON 数据转换为可用的结构以访问数据字段
  3. 重写为可测试的函数
  4. 实现自定义 UnmarshalJSON
  5. 结论

从 API 检索一些 JSON 数据时，我们不能像data.name那样调用这些属性。 将这些数据转换为结构需要一些解组、映射和接口。 因此，让我们通过一些 JSON 处理示例逐步实现我们的目标，即使来自 API 调用的数据可用。

您可以使用 VS Code 中的本地工具和 go-tools 执行这些步骤，或者直接使用Go Playground ，无需任何安装。

使用空接口打印未知 JSON 数据

为了获得一些示例数据，我们将一些来自 AI 微服务的 JSON 数据作为多行字符串读取到字节数组中。

 var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` )

接下来我们创建一个临时的空接口

var temp map [ string ] interface {}

然后将数据解组到这个变量 temp 中。 这需要导入„encoding/json“ 。

 json . Unmarshal ( data , & temp )

捕获任何解组错误

if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) }

然后我们可以打印出temp。

结果应该是

map [ predictions : [ map [ confidence : 0.69146144 label : Lion x_max : 186 x_min : 109 y_max : 707 y_min : 682 ] map [ confidence : 0.7 label : Penguin x_max : 196 x_min : 209 y_max : 717 y_min : 782 ]] success : true ]

伟大的。 如果我们只想访问标题

fmt . Println ( temp . predictions [ 0 ])

我们得到一个错误。

 ./ prog . go : 40 : 19 : temp . predictions undefined ( type map [ string ] interface {} has no field or method predictions )

好的，接下来让我们关注这个。 本节的清单。

 package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" ) func main () { var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) var temp map [ string ] interface {} //var prediction PredictionsAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & temp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } //fmt.Println(temp.predictions[0]) }

访问数据字段

假设我们事先知道预期的数据结构，让我们创建一个结构。

 type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` }

向上滚动到第一个 JSON 响应以查看其来源。 这反映了我们从 AI 微服务获得的响应。 要导出，名称必须以大写字母开头。 我们用两个函数Width()和Height()扩展PredictionsAdapter ，因为我们稍后需要这些信息来为我们的客户响应Predictions 。

 func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin }

我们没有将其解组为空接口，而是使用ResponseAdapter类型的变量。

 var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter json . Unmarshall ( data , & responseAdapter ) fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ])

这就是我们如何从 go as structs 访问 JSON 数据的方式:)。

 { 0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186 }

完整列表

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin } func main () { var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) //var temp map[string]interface{} var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) }

测试

我发现写一些有用的东西并首先看到一些结果总是很有动力。 为了适应良好的实践，让我们现在将所有内容重写为可测试的函数并摆脱main() 。 如果你使用 Go Playground，它会在没有main()的情况下自动运行测试函数。

首先导入"testing"和"github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" 。 它是一个want got与函数的实际结果进行比较的库。 它检查两者是否与if !cmp.Equal(want, got)相等，否则返回带有差异的错误。

我们从功能上下文中插入data声明，以便可以从每个测试功能访问它。

我们将在main中发生的大部分内容重命名为ParseImageAnalysisResponse ，它将接收[]byte类型的 JSON 内容并返回一个ResponseAdapter和一个error 。

测试函数具有相同的名称，带有前缀TestXxx和对*testing.T的引用，它可以使用go test在 go 中进行自动化测试。 然后第一行使用t.Parallel()启用并行测试执行。

然后我们声明我们的预期结果。 这次我们不使用 JSON 格式，因为我们期望 data 作为 go 结构。 这就是为什么我们在打开花括号之前声明类型的原因want := ResponseAdapter{... .}

 want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, }

然后我们必须用我们的data调用函数并捕获可能的错误。 最后我们比较期望和结果。

 if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) }

如果一切正确，我们可以使用 Run on Go Playground 或在命令行中进行go test ，测试应该通过或返回错误。

 === RUN TestParseImageAnalysisResponse === PAUSE TestParseImageAnalysisResponse === CONT TestParseImageAnalysisResponse { 0.69146144 Lion 682 109 707 186 } --- PASS : TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( 0.00 s ) PASS

完整列表

package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data [] byte ) ( ResponseAdapter , error ) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) return responseAdapter , nil } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }

现在我们有了可以实际使用的格式的数据。 我们甚至可以将其转换为 JSON 并将其传递给我们的用户客户端。 但有时实现逻辑需要我们添加或删除一些字段，并以不同的格式提供数据。 如果这是最有意义的，我们应该这样做以维护一个设计良好的数据工作流，即使它需要一些额外的工作。

在下一节中，我们将以我们实际需要的方式定义Response和Predictions类型，并使用自定义UnmarshallJSON从我们的 Adapter 类型转换它们。 首先，我们将添加一些自定义元数据。 但是，在[]PredictionsAdapter中添加或更改字段需要遍历数组中的每个元素。

实现自定义 UnmarshallJSON

让我们为接下来要实现的ParseImageAnalysisResponse创建测试（忽略任何错误）。 我们使用数据将其传递给ParseImageAnalysisResponse ，然后查看是否满足我们的期望。

 func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := Response { Name : "Test Image" , Xid : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" , Predictions : [] Prediction { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , X : 109 , Y : 682 , Width : 77 , Height : 25 , Method : "AI" , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , X : 209 , Y : 782 , Width : - 13 , Height : - 65 , Method : "AI" , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }

接下来，我们定义与我们想要中继到客户端的 JSON API 响应相匹配的类型，可以是 Web 应用程序或 Android 或 iOS 的移动应用程序。

 type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?) }

响应将包含许多这些预测，因为可以在图像中检测到许多对象。 但更进一步，它将包括元数据，如Name和Xid 。 稍后我们可能需要更改类型，但现在让我们使用string 。

 type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions [] Prediction `json:"predictions"` }

现在我们将调整ParseImageAnalysisResponse函数。 它仍将使用[]byte类型的data作为参数，但它现在将返回我们的新Response ResponseAdapter 因此，我们使用Response类型的变量response并在解组时使用它。 然后我们检查错误并返回response 。

 func ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data [] byte ) ( Response , error ) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & response ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } return response , nil }

由于从ResponseAdapter到Response没有简单的一对一映射，我们需要做一些手动工作。 代替我们不必指定的默认UnmarshalJSON ，我们现在将使用它的自定义版本，明确说明我们如何从Response中的数据派生对客户端的ResponseAdapter 。
我们发送给ParseImageAnalysisResponse的data是我们想要解组的数据。 我们知道我们可以将它解组为一个ResponseAdapter ，所以这就是我们首先要做的，但不是解组到响应对象r的指针中，而是解组到一个临时变量tmp中。

 var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) }

此变量还具有 PredictionsAdapter 形式的PredictionsAdapter 。 我们现在将它们转换成一个新的 Predictions-Array。

我们通过循环每个元素PredictionsAdapter来做到这一点

for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions {

并创建一个临时对应的预测元素。
这个 predictions 元素还定义了任何添加的字段的数据，或者它也可以从PredictionsAdapter中省略字段。 然后我们将 Predictions 元素附加到在循环之前定义的 Predictions Array p中。 我在这里一步完成了变量创建和附加，并关闭了循环。

 p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , XMin : element . XMin , XMax : element . XMax , YMin : element . YMin , YMax : element . YMax , }) }

然后我们设置Response字段。 这包括名称和 Xid 等元数据以及设置预测数组 p。

 r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p

最后，由于 UnmarshalJSON 要求我们返回错误，我们也这样做。

现在让我们看看这个函数的完整外观。

 func ( r * Response ) UnmarshalJSON ( data [] byte ) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } var p [] Prediction for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions { p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , X : element . XMin , Y : element . YMin , Width : element . Width (), Height : element . Height (), }) } r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p return err }

结论

伟大的。 我们现在可以拍摄一张带有 AI 微服务注释的图像，并通过使用更相关的 JSON 响应数据对其进行增强，从而使该分析对于将其转发给用户客户端很有用。 在网络方面，解释了如何将来自 HTTP 请求的 JSON 响应准确地发送到客户端。 我在上一篇文章中谈到了如何向 AI 微服务发送 POST 请求。

最后，让我们看看完整的清单，您也可以在Go Playground上找到它。 当然，测试也可以写入单独的文件。

感谢阅读，我希望你觉得这很有用。 这是我新博客上的前五篇文章之一，所以我感谢任何意见和建议。

 package main import ( "encoding/json" "fmt" "github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp" "testing" ) type ResponseAdapter struct { Success bool `json:"success"` Predictions [] PredictionsAdapter `json:"predictions"` } type PredictionsAdapter struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` YMin int `json:"y_min"` XMin int `json:"x_min"` YMax int `json:"y_max"` XMax int `json:"x_max"` } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Width () int { return p . XMax - p . XMin } func ( p PredictionsAdapter ) Height () int { return p . YMax - p . YMin } type Prediction struct { Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"` Label string `json:"label"` X int `json:"x"` Y int `json:"y"` Width int `json:"width"` Height int `json:"height"` Method string `json:"method"` // AI, Manual, Predicted(?) } type Response struct { Name string `json:"name"` Xid string `json:"xid"` Predictions [] Prediction `json:"predictions"` } func ( r * Response ) UnmarshalJSON ( data [] byte ) error { var tmp ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & tmp ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } var p [] Prediction for _ , element := range tmp . Predictions { // fmt.Println("element", element, "at index", index) p = append ( p , Prediction { Confidence : element . Confidence , Method : "AI" , Label : element . Label , X : element . XMin , Y : element . YMin , Width : element . Width (), Height : element . Height (), }) } r . Name = "Test Image" r . Xid = "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" r . Predictions = p return err } var data = [] byte ( `{"success": true, "predictions": [ { "confidence": 0.69146144, "label": "Lion", "y_min": 682, "x_min": 109, "y_max": 707, "x_max": 186 }, { "confidence": 0.7, "label": "Penguin", "y_min": 782, "x_min": 209, "y_max": 717, "x_max": 196 }] }` ) func ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data [] byte ) ( ResponseAdapter , error ) { var responseAdapter ResponseAdapter err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & responseAdapter ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } fmt . Println ( responseAdapter . Predictions [ 0 ]) return responseAdapter , nil } func ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data [] byte ) ( Response , error ) { var response Response // Use custom UnmarshalJSON method to obtain Response err := json . Unmarshal ( data , & response ) if err != nil { fmt . Println ( err ) } // fmt.Println("Response") // fmt.Println(response) return response , nil } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := ResponseAdapter { Success : true , Predictions : [] PredictionsAdapter { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , YMin : 682 , XMin : 109 , YMax : 707 , XMax : 186 , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , YMin : 782 , XMin : 209 , YMax : 717 , XMax : 196 , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponseAdapter ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } } func TestParseImageAnalysisResponse ( t * testing . T ) { t . Parallel () want := Response { Name : "Test Image" , Xid : "9m4e2mr0ui3e8a215n4g" , Predictions : [] Prediction { { Confidence : 0.69146144 , Label : "Lion" , X : 109 , Y : 682 , Width : 77 , Height : 25 , Method : "AI" , }, { Confidence : 0.7 , Label : "Penguin" , X : 209 , Y : 782 , Width : - 13 , Height : - 65 , Method : "AI" , }, }, } got , err := ParseImageAnalysisResponse ( data ) if err != nil { t . Fatal ( err ) } if ! cmp . Equal ( want , got ) { t . Error ( cmp . Diff ( want , got )) } }
